findings.claim: ADR-001, ADR-003, and ADR-007 each engage the same unresolved question — when the legacy SDKv2 resources under proxmoxtf/ will be fully retired and rewritten under the Plugin Framework with the new proxmox_ naming — and none of the three records closes that question. ADR-001 prioritizes VM and Container for migration but explicitly leaves SDKv2 usable 'until they are migrated'; ADR-003 documents the 'old' proxmox_virtual_environment_ naming as still-live legacy alongside the new convention; and ADR-007 explicitly defers the actual SDK-to-Framework rewrite to a future v1.0 breaking-change phase that has not landed within the record. The checker counts this as a 3-record chain that banks partial progress (naming Phase 1/2, migration priority) without resolving the underlying retirement of SDKv2 resources.

citations.quote: Bug fixes to existing SDKv2 resources (until they are migrated)
SDK resources (`proxmoxtf/`) are feature-frozen. As part of v1.0, each SDK resource is rewritten in the Framework provider with the new `proxmox_*` name directly.

findings.consequence: If this assessment is correct, a maintainer reviewing the ADR log should treat the SDKv2-to-Framework retirement as still an open decision rather than something ADR-007 finalized, and should confirm whether the v1.0 Phase 3 rewrite has since actually shipped before relying on any of these three records as the settled state of the migration.
